name: CME Group
overview_text: CME Group is the world's leading and most diverse derivatives marketplace. Its exchanges offer futures, options, cash and over-the-counter markets in interest rates, equity indexes, foreign exchange, energy, agricultural commodities, metals and other products.
headquarters_address: 20 S. Wacker Dr. Chicago, IL 60606 United States
history_timeline: {'1898': 'Founded as the Chicago Mercantile Exchange (CME) by CME member firms in a hotel room in Chicago.', '2000': 'Acquired the Chicago Board of Trade (CBOT) and the Chicago Board Options Exchange (CBOE).', '2017': 'Acquired NEX Group in a deal worth $5.4 billion.'}

core_value_proposition: Efficient and secure markets for buying and selling financial contracts.

description: CME Group is the world's leading and most diverse derivatives marketplace. Its exchanges offer futures, options, cash and over-the-counter markets in interest rates, equity indexes, foreign exchange, energy, agricultural commodities, metals and other products.
vision_statement: Empowering global markets through innovation, collaboration, and integrity.
mission_statement: To be the leading provider of global markets, financial data and analytics, and trading technology.
sales_motion: CME Group offers a range of products and services, including futures and options trading, clearing and settlement, and market data, to help customers navigate complex markets and manage risk.
exit_strategy_history: CME Group has a history of successful acquisitions and divestitures, including the acquisition of the Chicago Board of Trade in 2007 and the divestiture of its interest in the NYMEX in 2008.
carbon_footprint: CME Group has set a goal to be carbon neutral by 2025 and has made significant reductions in its greenhouse gas emissions in recent years.
ethical_sourcing: CME Group has a strong commitment to ethical sourcing and has implemented policies and procedures to ensure that its suppliers adhere to high standards of ethics and sustainability.
benchmark_vs_peers: CME Group has consistently outperformed its peers in terms of revenue growth, profitability, and market share.
industry_associations: CME Group is a member of several industry associations, including the Securities Industry and Financial Markets Association (SIFMA) and the Futures Industry Association (FIA).
case_studies: CME Group's successful launch of its Bitcoin futures contract in 2017
CME Group's implementation of its clearing and settlement system for interest rate swaps
sustainability_csr: CME Group has a strong commitment to sustainability and corporate social responsibility and has implemented several initiatives to reduce its environmental impact and promote social responsibility.
crisis_behavior: CME Group has a strong track record of responding effectively to crises, including the 2008 financial crisis and the COVID-19 pandemic.
